reasoning:
The stock experienced a significant drop following its Q3 earnings release on Oct 31, falling from ~$226 to a low of ~$210. This was driven by a $2.7 billion one-time IPR&D charge that severely affected GAAP earnings. However, the underlying fundamentals were strong: adjusted EPS beat consensus expectations and the company raised its full-year 2025 adjusted EPS guidance. The market typically buys the dip on positive adjusted guidance following a one-time accounting hit, which is exactly what appears to be happening — the stock has rallied for 4 consecutive days prior to the Nov 9 cutoff, reaching 217.4. 

For the short-term resolution date (Nov 16), this "mean reversion" momentum toward pre-earnings levels provides a bullish signal, justifying a probability above the baseline. For the medium-term date (Dec 9), this specific momentum signal fades slightly. For the furthest date (Feb 7), the standard equity drift is slightly counteracted by an expected ex-dividend date in mid-January, which will temporarily drop the stock price by approximately $1.64 (~0.75%). Following the instructions, uncertainty increases and the probability regresses closer to 0.5 for the more distant horizons.
